Description: A viral infection of your nose and throat.
column8: Rhino viruses attach to the nasal lining. This triggers your immune system to release inflammatory chemicals (histamines) that dilate blood vessels and cause mucus buildup

Description: A neurological condition causing severe headaches.
column8: Abnormal brain activity temporarily affects nerve signals and chemical balances (like serotonin). This causes blood vessels in the brain to swell and inflame
column9: sending intense pain signals to the nervous system.

column8: Ingesting contaminated food or water introduces viruses (like Rotavirus) or bacteria (like E. coli) into the gut. These pathogens attack the stomach and intestinal lining
column9: preventing water absorption and causing inflammation.
